target: Erdős Problem 142 — r_k(N) asymptotics WIT spec
notes: Step 5 (rk_three_eq_rothNumberNat) was proved by the concurrent bridge worker (sess_ee9df00e) whose changes appeared in the file during this run. Steps 6, 7, 8 were proved in this session.

step_6_rk_le_N.lean_theorem: theorem rk_le_N (k N : ℕ) : rk k N ≤ N
step_6_rk_le_N.proof_strategy: unfold rk; letI := Classical.decPred _; exact Nat.findGreatest_le N

step_7_rk_mono_N.claim: N ≤ M → rk k N ≤ rk k M
step_7_rk_mono_N.lean_theorem: theorem rk_mono_N (k : ℕ) {N M : ℕ} (h : N ≤ M) : rk k N ≤ rk k M
step_7_rk_mono_N.proof_strategy: unfold rk; letI for both predicates; apply Nat.findGreatest_mono; transport AP-free set via Finset.range_mono h

step_8_rk_pos.lean_theorem: theorem rk_pos (k : ℕ) (hk : 3 ≤ k) (N : ℕ) (hN : 1 ≤ N) : 0 < rk k N
step_8_rk_pos.proof_strategy: exhibit singleton {0} ⊆ range N as k-AP-free witness (size 1); use Nat.le_findGreatest; ArithProg.not_hasKAP_singleton 0 (hk ≥ 2)

implementation_notes: Critical fix: use 'letI' (transparent let binding) not 'haveI' for DecidablePred instances, to avoid Classical.decPred opacity mismatch
For rk_mono_N, Nat.findGreatest_mono handles both predicate and bound change simultaneously
For rk_pos, ArithProg.not_hasKAP_singleton (k ≥ 2) is available from ArithmeticProgressions.lean
Step 5 proof (rk_three_eq_rothNumberNat) uses bridge: HasKAP A 3 ↔ ¬ThreeAPFree (A : Set ℕ), proved in detail in BridgeProofAttempt.lean and merged into RkN.lean
